title: Electrical Tools and Meter Skills Trainer
summary: Learners work through realistic measuring tasks using hand tools, multimeters, clamp meters, and continuity testers while following correct mode and lead placement rules.
learningObjectives: Select the correct tool for common electrical tasks.
Use meter modes safely for voltage, resistance, continuity, and current checks.
Recognize common measurement mistakes before they become hazards.

environment.setting: Service bench with locked tool drawers, energized and de-energized test boards, and digital instrument rack
environment.focus: Safe tool selection and disciplined measurement habits

interactables.label: Digital multimeter
interactables.teaches: Covers lead placement, rotary mode selection, and safe measurement sequence.

interactables.label: Clamp meter
interactables.teaches: Shows non-contact current measurement on a single conductor.

interactables.label: Continuity tester
interactables.teaches: Reinforces that continuity tests belong on de-energized circuits only.

interactables.label: Insulated tool rack
interactables.teaches: Compares cutters, drivers, strippers, and torque tools for the job at hand.

tasks: Choose the correct tool for stripping, tightening, measuring, and verifying tasks.
Set the multimeter to the correct mode before probing the circuit.
Use the clamp meter on a single conductor and read the current value.
Identify and correct an unsafe meter setup before taking a reading.
completion: Learner selects the correct tool in every scenario.
Learner demonstrates proper meter setup and probe sequence.
